title: Find the Largest Element
description:
Write a method `findMax` that takes an array of integers and returns the largest value.

Given the array `{23, 7, 42, 15, 38, 4}`, your method should return 42.

**Rules:**
- Do NOT use Arrays.sort() or any library methods
- Use a loop to compare each element
hints: Start by assuming the first element is the max: int max = arr[0];
Loop through the rest of the array (start at index 1) and update max whenever you find something bigger
Use an if statement: if (arr[i] > max) { max = arr[i]; }

title: Reverse an Array
description:
Write a method `reverse` that takes an array of integers and reverses it **in place** (modifying the original array, not creating a new one).

Given `{1, 2, 3, 4, 5}`, the array should become `{5, 4, 3, 2, 1}`.

Print each element separated by a space to verify.

**Hint:** Swap elements from the outside in — swap first with last, second with second-to-last, etc.
hints: Use two pointers: one starting at the beginning (left = 0) and one at the end (right = arr.length - 1)
Swap arr[left] and arr[right], then move both pointers inward (left++, right--)
Stop when left >= right — you've met in the middle. Use a temp variable for swapping!

title: Count Even Numbers
description:
Write a method `countEvens` that takes an array of integers and returns how many of them are even.

Given `{3, 8, 12, 5, 7, 20, 1, 4}`, the method should return 4 (because 8, 12, 20, and 4 are even).

**Reminder:** A number is even if `number % 2 == 0`.
hints: Create a counter variable starting at 0
Use an enhanced for loop: for (int num : arr)
Inside the loop, check if num % 2 == 0 and increment the counter if so

title: Linear Search
description:
Write a method `linearSearch` that takes an array of integers and a target value. It should return the **index** of the first occurrence of the target, or **-1** if the target is not found.

Test with:
- Searching for 30 in `{10, 20, 30, 40, 50}` → should print 2
- Searching for 99 in the same array → should print -1
hints: Use a regular for loop (you need the index!) — for (int i = 0; i < arr.length; i++)
If arr[i] == target, return i immediately — you found it!
If the loop finishes without finding the target, return -1 after the loop

title: Remove Duplicates
description:
Write a method `removeDuplicates` that takes a sorted array of integers and returns a NEW array containing only the unique values (no duplicates).

Given `{1, 1, 2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 5, 5}`, return `{1, 2, 3, 4, 5}`.

Print the result array elements separated by spaces.

**Challenge:** The input array is sorted, which makes this easier — duplicates are always next to each other!
hints: First pass: count the unique elements by checking if arr[i] != arr[i-1]
Create a new array of that size, then do a second pass to fill it with unique values
Since the array is sorted, a value is unique (not a duplicate) if it's different from the one before it. The first element is always unique.
